title: Dirac Semimetal Phase in Rhombohedral $β-$Cu$_{2}$Se
authors: Thomas Steele, Becker Sharif, David Lederman, Xiangang Wan, Sergey Y. Savrasov
abstract: Having been extensively studied during last decades in the fields of thermoelectics and ionic conductors, the $α$ phase of Cu$_{2}$Se with antfluoride crystal structure has recently emerged as a topological zero-gap semimetal with a quadratic contact point which exists at the Fermi surface of its bulk electronic spectrum. Here we argue based on density functional electronic structure calculation that the $β$ phase of Cu$_{2}$Se realized in a recently discovered rhombohedral structure shows a Dirac semimetal behavior of the electrons near the Fermi level. These topological semimetals are currently generating a lot of interest due to unusual transport phenomena, such as strong quantum oscillations, large magnetoresistance effect and ultrahigh carrier mobilities with their Fermi velocities potentially exceeding graphene. We show that there exist Fermi arc states at the surface spectrum of $β-$Cu$_{2}$Se that are topologically protected by the bulk Dirac points. Their shape and spin properties should be resilient to the back- and side scattering effects in the surface transport, suggesting new ways for realizing high-mobility electronic devices.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the rhombohedral beta-phase of Cu2Se is a Dirac semimetal, distinguishing it from the quadratic contact point found in the cubic alpha-phase. It predicts the existence of topologically protected Fermi arcs with a spin texture that suppresses backscattering, suggesting potential for high-mobility electronic devices.
main_problem: Determining whether the rhombohedral beta-phase of Cu2Se realizes a Dirac semimetal state and identifying the presence of topologically protected Fermi arc surface states.
main_result: The study predicts that the beta-phase of Cu2Se is a Dirac semimetal with protected Dirac points and surface Fermi arcs that exhibit a helical spin texture, potentially leading to ultrahigh carrier mobility due to suppressed backscattering.
method: Density Functional Theory (DFT) using the Full-potential Linear Muffin-Tin Orbital (FP-LMTO) method with spin-orbit coupling, and a tight-binding approach for surface slab calculations.
model_or_system: The physical system is the rhombohedral beta-phase of Cu2Se, a non-stoichiometric material with space-group symmetry R3m, which can be viewed as a strained version of the cubic alpha-phase.
key_observables: Dirac dispersions, Fermi arc surface states, spin texture (helical-like), and carrier mobility.
important_parameters: Lattice parameters (a = b = 4.12 A, c = 20.45 A), c/a ratio of 4.963, and the presence of inversion and time-reversal symmetry.
main_assumptions: The transport discussion assumes a thin-film geometry where bulk contributions are negligible, and surface calculations use a tight-binding transformation rather than full self-consistent slab calculations.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the crystal structures of the alpha and beta phases; Figure 2 displays the calculated band structures for both phases; Figure 3 illustrates the Brillouin Zone with Dirac points and the calculated Fermi arc surface states on the (100) surface.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the structural difference between alpha and beta Cu2Se, presents DFT calculations of the bulk electronic structure, compares the two phases, details the surface state calculations, and discusses the implications for transport properties.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: a) Antifluoride crystal structure of α−Cu2Se oriented along (111) direction of the cubic lattice; The lattice trans- lations for the original unit cell, a1, a2, a3, are indicated. b) Rhombohedral crystal structure of β−Cu2Se derived from the cubic phase by choosing z–axis along (111), rearranging the interlayer spacing between Se atoms which results in doubling the unit cell of the cubic phase with new lattice translations A1, A2, A3, and additional displacements of Cu atoms from their ideal tetrahedral interstitials[15].

FIG. 2: a) Calculated band structure of the β phase of Cu2Se along high–symmetry lines of the rhombohedral Bril- louin Zone. b) Energy band dispersions of the cubic α−Cu2Se drawn in the rhombohedral Brillouin Zone by utilizing z axis along (111) and by doubling the unit cell to make direct com- parisons between the bands of α and β phases possible.

FIG. 3: a) Brillouin Zone of the rhombohedral lattice of β−Cu2Se with high-symmetry points indicated. The Dirac points occur along kz direction and are indicated by blue dots. b) Calculated Fermi arc surface states for β−Cu2Se that are shown in the surface Brillouin Zone corresponding to the (100) surface.
